name: Escape Analysis
def: Compiler/runtime analysis determining whether an object outlives its scope, enabling stack allocation.
problem: Avoid heap allocation (and GC pressure) for objects that never escape their creating method.
mechanism: If an object provably does not escape (not returned, stored globally, or shared), the compiler can stack-allocate it, scalar-replace its fields, or elide locks.
limits: Opaque (hard to know if it fired)
failures: Relying on it for predictable performance
Patterns that defeat the analysis (escaping refs)
whenNot: Cannot be controlled directly; write escape-friendly code
myths: "The JVM stack-allocates objects" — only via escape analysis, and not guaranteed
